channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: What Time Does Rocket League Season 23 Start?
item.content:encoded:
It’s not long now until Rocket League Season 23 starts with a huge and exciting update to the game.

Rocket League Season 23 will be World Cup-themed with free Octane Decals for all 48 participating countries.

And the update will also include multiple quality-of-life improvements, including the ability to choose which side you respawn after a demo.

There will also be the addition of various features, like Hitbox Visualization and Ball Speed Graphs, that players used to have with Bakkesmod before the anti-cheat system was enabled.

Rocket League Season 23 Global Release Times

The new Rocket League season is starting just in time for the start of the World Cup, releasing on Wednesday, June 10.

The exact date and release time for the Rocket League Season 22 update in every major region is as follows:

  • Pacific Time (PDT) – 09:00 June 10
  • Mountain Time (MDT) – 10:00 June 10
  • Central Time (CDT) – 11:00 June 10
  • Eastern Time (EDT) – 12:00 June 10
  • British Summer Time (BST) – 17:00 June 10
  • Eastern European Time (EEST) – 19:00 June 10
  • Moscow Standard Time (MSK) – 19:00 June 10
  • Indian Standard Time (IST) – 21:30 June 10
  • China Standard Time (CST) – 00:00 June 11
  • Japan Standard Time (JST) – 01:00 June 11
  • Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST) – 02:00 June 11
  • New Zealand Standard Time (NZST) – 04:00 June 11

Once the season launches, Rocket League players will have until July 13, 9:00 AM PT to claim a free Octane Decal and Title for the World Cup country they choose to support.

Then, after the IRL football tournament concludes, players will receive the 47 other World Cup Decals plus a title for the winning country.

Are you excited about the recent updates Psyonix and Epic Games have released for Rocket League? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And get ready for an even bigger update, as a future Unreal Engine 6 upgrade for Rocket League has been confirmed.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/what-time-does-rocket-league-season-23-start/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Esports World Cup Last Chance Qualifiers Details Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
The Esports World Cup 2026 is just a month away, and now the Esports Foundation has revealed more about the Last Chance Qualifiers.

This year’s Esports World Cup moved to Paris, France and will feature 25 tournaments across 24 different gaming titles.

According to a press release, qualification for the Esports World Cup this year has spanned over 230 tournaments with more than 350k competitors.

And now, the final participants are about to be decided with Last Chance Qualifiers for Counter-Strike 2, EA FC, Rocket League, TEKKEN 8, Chess, Call of Duty: Black Ops 7, FATAL FURY: City of the Wolves, and Street Fighter 6.

Esports World Cup Last Chance Qualifier Slots

While most teams’ EWC qualification is now locked in, there is still room for 37 individual players and six clubs (teams) to qualify.

The Last Chance Qualifiers are open-registration, on-site events during the Esports World Cup to decide the last participants.

The LCQs will see four clubs qualify for the CS2 tournament, and one final team for both Rocket League and CoD Black Ops 7.

The EA FC Qualifiers will feed into the Play-Ins stage of the event, while the fighting games and Chess LCQs will feed directly into each game’s main events.

The schedule and slots of each EWC LCQ are as follows:

  • FATAL FURY: City of the Wolves (July 5th): Four players qualify 
  • EA FC (July 9-11): 14 players qualify for Play-Ins
  • Street Fighter 6 (July 24-26): Six players qualify
  • TEKKEN 8 (July 31-August 2): Four players qualify
  • Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 (July 31-August 2): One Club qualifies
  • Chess (August  4-6): Four players qualify
  • Counter-Strike 2 (August 7-9): Four Clubs qualify
  • Rocket League (August 8-August 10): One Club qualifies

Are there any qualified players or teams you’re rooting for? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, Karmine Corp was reinstated in the EWC Club Partnership Program after Heroic dropped out.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Has Recast Sephiroth
item.content:encoded:
The English voice actor for Sephiroth has been recast for Final Fantasy 7: Revelation, with someone from Critical Role taking over the role.

Summer Game Fest 2026 didn’t disappoint Final Fantasy fans, with Square Enix not only revealing Part 3: Revelation, but also providing a Spring 2027 release window for PS5, PC, Switch 2, and Xbox Series X/S. Now, one doubt that fans had from the reveal trailer has been clarified.

Final Fantasy 7 Revelation Brings in Critical Role Actor for Sephiroth

In a recent interview with Maxmillian Dood (via Polygon), Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director Naoki Hamaguchi was asked if they had cast a new English voice actor for Sephiroth, to which he confirmed that they had. Hamaguchi was first surprised that players realized, then proceeded to provide a reason. The director stated that the former English VA for Sephiroth, Tyler Hoechlin, “was unable to return with his voice” for the new part, so they had to cast a new actor.

Sometime later, Maximillian Dood received an update from Square Enix, which allowed him to reveal the new English VA for Sephiroth. He confirmed that the new actor is Travis Willingham, who has lent his voice for a whole lot of characters across a bunch of games, including Infamous Second Son, Saints Row, Star Wars Jedi: Fallen Order, Marvel’s Spider-Man, Marvel Rivals, Dispatch, and so many more.

In other news, Final Fantasy 7 Revelation director has revealed that the game does have choices, but will feature a ‘singular ending’. Additionally, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3’s director has confirmed that they have left the door open for DLC. What are your thoughts on the new voice actor for Sephiroth in Final Fantasy 7 Revelation? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about XBOX exclusivity being decided ‘case by case’. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
